message.content:
{
  "competency_groups": [
    {
      "code": "C-1.1",
      "title": "Understanding Numbers and Real Number System",
      "description": "Develops understanding of numbers, including the set of real numbers and its properties.",
      "competencies": [
        "Understand the concept of a rational number.",
        "Represent rational numbers on the number line.",
        "Understand the properties of rational numbers.",
        "Explain the concept of density of rational numbers.",
        "Compute decimal representation of rational numbers.",
        "Understand the concept of irrational numbers.",
        "Prove the irrationality.",
        "Construct the square root spiral."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Number System",
      "chapter_name": "Number System"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-3.1, C-3.2",
      "title": "Algebraic Reasoning and Modelling",
      "description": "States and proves remainder theorem, factor theorem, division algorithm; models real-life situations in the form of equations.",
      "competencies": [
        "Understand the meaning of an algebraic expression.",
        "Define a polynomial.",
        "Identify the degree, terms and coefficients of terms in a polynomial.",
        "Model linear growth and decay using linear polynomials.",
        "Explain and identify patterns in linear relationships.",
        "Identify the slope and y-intercept of a linear equation in two variables.",
        "Graph a linear equation in two variables."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Algebra",
      "chapter_name": "Introduction to Polynomials"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-3.2, C-8.1",
      "title": "Sequences and Computational Thinking",
      "description": "Models daily-life phenomena using representations; uses computational thinking for pattern recognition.",
      "competencies": [
        "Understand the concept of a sequence of numbers.",
        "Identify the pattern in a sequence and predict the next few terms.",
        "Determine the recursive and explicit rules for different sequences.",
        "Obtain the terms of sequence given its recursive and explicit rule.",
        "Identify Arithmetic Progressions (AP).",
        "Determine the nth term of an AP.",
        "Visualise an AP graphically.",
        "Identify Geometric Progressions (GP).",
        "Determine the nth term of a GP.",
        "Visualise a GP graphically.",
        "Analyse attributes of fractals using GP."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Algebra",
      "chapter_name": "Sequences and Progressions"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-7.2, C-9.1",
      "title": "Visualisation and Decomposition of Identities",
      "description": "Uses computational thinking strategies such as decomposition to visualise identities.",
      "competencies": [
        "Visualise algebraic identities using geometric models.",
        "Determine the factors of algebraic expressions using identities.",
        "Interpret factors of quadratic expressions through geometric models.",
        "Find simplified versions of rational expressions."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Algebra",
      "chapter_name": "Exploring Algebraic Identities"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-3.2, C-8.1",
      "title": "Linear Equations and Graphical Modelling",
      "description": "Models daily-life phenomena using representations; solves contextualised problems.",
      "competencies": [
        "Understand the concept of a linear equation in two variables.",
        "Graph a pair of linear equations.",
        "Solve a pair of linear equations graphically.",
        "Solve a pair of linear equations through substitution and elimination methods.",
        "Determine the nature of solutions of a pair of linear equations."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Algebra",
      "chapter_name": "Linear Equations in Two Variables"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-4.5, C-9.1",
      "title": "Coordinate Geometry and Spatial Reasoning",
      "description": "Specifies locations using coordinates; applies computational thinking to model situations.",
      "competencies": [
        "Specify locations and the position of one point relative to another point using coordinates.",
        "Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates.",
        "Compute the distance between two points using coordinates.",
        "Determine whether three points lie in a straight line using coordinates.",
        "Compute the position of the midpoint of a line segment using coordinates."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Coordinate Geometry",
      "chapter_name": "Coordinate Geometry"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-7.1, C-7.3",
      "title": "Axiomatic Structure and Constructions",
      "description": "Proves mathematical statements using axioms; carries out geometric constructions.",
      "competencies": [
        "Describe how geometry grew from practical needs of ancient civilisations.",
        "Understand the role of definitions, axioms, and postulates.",
        "State the 5 postulates of Euclidean geometry.",
        "Define parallelism of straight lines.",
        "Justify simple constructions using the axioms."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Geometry",
      "chapter_name": "Introduction to Euclid's Geometry"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-7.1, C-9.2",
      "title": "Lines and Angles: Logical Reasoning",
      "description": "Follows proof by contradiction; applies computational thinking to analyse geometric ideas.",
      "competencies": [
        "Explain the notion of an angle and a ray.",
        "Classify angles as acute, right, obtuse, or reflex.",
        "State and apply linear pair theorem and its converse.",
        "Prove that vertically opposite angles are equal.",
        "Identify corresponding, alternate, and interior angles."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Geometry",
      "chapter_name": "Lines and Angles"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-4.1, C-7.3",
      "title": "Triangle Congruence Theorems",
      "description": "Proves theorems using Euclid's axioms; explains propositions and their converses.",
      "competencies": [
        "Explain that a triangle is rigid.",
        "Describe what it means for two triangles to be congruent.",
        "Use SAS, SSS, ASA, RHS, AAS congruence conditions.",
        "Prove basic properties of isosceles triangles.",
        "Identify the converse of a given proposition."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Geometry",
      "chapter_name": "Triangles: Congruence Theorems"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-4.2, C-7.3",
      "title": "Quadrilaterals and Symmetry",
      "description": "Proves theorems related to parallelograms; understands central symmetry.",
      "competencies": [
        "Frame a precise definition of a 4-gon.",
        "Prove various characterisations of a parallelogram.",
        "Prove the midpoint theorem and its converse.",
        "Understand reflection and rotation symmetries of 4-gons."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Geometry",
      "chapter_name": "Quadrilaterals"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-7.3, C-9.1",
      "title": "Circle Geometry and Properties",
      "description": "Proves theorems about circles; uses computational thinking to verify properties.",
      "competencies": [
        "State the definition of a circle.",
        "Explain meanings of chord, diameter, radius, arc, segment, sector.",
        "Construct circumcircle and circumcentre of a triangle.",
        "Prove relationships between chords, angles at centre, and cyclic quadrilaterals."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Geometry",
      "chapter_name": "Circles"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-5.1, C-9.3",
      "title": "Mensuration: Area and Perimeter",
      "description": "Derives formulae for areas; uses computational thinking to break down shapes.",
      "competencies": [
        "Define perimeter as the length around a shape.",
        "Compute circumference of a circle and arc length.",
        "Apply Heron's formula to compute area from sides.",
        "Explain Brahmagupta's formula for cyclic quadrilaterals."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Mensuration",
      "chapter_name": "Area and Perimeter"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-5.1, C-9.4",
      "title": "Surface Area and Volume Calculations",
      "description": "Derives formulae for surface areas and volumes; uses computational thinking to compare shapes.",
      "competencies": [
        "Compute the surface area and volume of cuboids, cubes, cylinders, cones, pyramids, spheres."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Mensuration",
      "chapter_name": "Surface Area and Volume"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-6.1, C-9.3",
      "title": "Statistical Analysis and Data Interpretation",
      "description": "Applies measures of central tendency; uses computational thinking to analyse real-life data.",
      "competencies": [
        "Collect, organise, visualise and interpret data.",
        "Compute weighted average, mean, median, mode."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Statistics",
      "chapter_name": "Statistics"
    },
    {
      "code": "C-6.2, C-9.4",
      "title": "Probability and Randomness Modelling",
      "description": "Applies concepts from probability; uses computational thinking to model random experiments.",
      "competencies": [
        "Understand the concept of randomness.",
        "Describe likelihood using probability scale.",
        "Estimate empirical probability by analysing data.",
        "Define and compute theoretical probability."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Statistics",
      "chapter_name": "Introduction to Probability"
    }
  ]
}
